origin.name: Spain
origin.post_mortem_trigger: All sudden, unattended, or traumatic deaths
origin.island_transfer_note: Deaths on the Balearic or Canary Islands require internal transfer to a mainland airport before international cargo can depart. Add 1-3 days.
origin.source: FCDO Spain travel advice, updated June 2025

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Certificado de defuncion (death certificate from Registro Civil)

airlines.name: British Airways
airlines.routes: MAD, BCN, AGP, ALC to LHR

special_rules: Juzgado de Instruccion takes jurisdiction for all sudden, unattended, traumatic, or unexplained deaths. Cannot be bypassed. Adds 5-15 working days.
Island deaths (Balearics, Canaries) require internal mainland transfer. Add 1-3 days.
Only one original certificado de defuncion is issued. Guard it carefully.
A multilingual EU death certificate is not automatically issued - must be requested.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Spanish documentation must be in certified English. The coroner for the district where the funeral will take place is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements. Juzgado investigation cases may require coroner review.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The FCDO can provide information and a list of local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Spain to UK Repatriation: Guidance for British Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Spain. Repatriation to the UK takes 7-14 days in a straightforward case. Juzgado process, island transfers, and all documentation handled. Contact us 24/7.

destinations.name: Ireland

airlines.name: Ryanair cargo
airlines.routes: AGP, MAD, BCN to DUB, ORK

special_rules: Juzgado de Instruccion applies for all sudden or unattended deaths. Cannot be bypassed.
Island deaths require mainland transfer before international departure.
Irish Embassy in Madrid is the consular contact, not the British Embassy.
destinations.dest_reception: The Irish funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Spanish documentation must be in certified English. The Coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed to funeral arrangements without delay.
destinations.dest_consular_note: Department of Foreign Affairs emergency line: +353 1 408 2000 (24 hours). The Irish Embassy in Madrid can register the death and advise on documentation. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Spain to Ireland: Funeral Repatriation Guidance
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Spain and needs to come home to Ireland. Repatriation takes 7-14 days. Juzgado process, island transfers, and all documentation handled. Contact us.
